title: Ionidium lineatum
taxonomy: Ionidium lineatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is native to Asia and is a type of shrub that produces small, white flowers. As a member of the Ericaceae family, Ionidium lineatum is classified as a member of the order Ericales. This classification groups it with other plants that share similar characteristics, such as the presence of small flowers and woody stems. The Ericaceae family is a diverse group that includes many species of shrubs and trees found in temperate and tropical regions.

intro: Ionidium lineatum, also known as the Yellow Jasmine, is a flowering plant native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its fragrant, star-shaped flowers and woody climbing vines. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and hypertension, due to its calming and sedative effects. The bioactive constituents of Ionidium lineatum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies for its purported health benefits.
